Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for Jordan Whitfield's complaints and confirmation via two calls: search_emails(query="Jordan Whitfield", folder_name="INBOX") and search_emails(query="Jordan Whitfield", folder_name="SENT"). The INBOX search returns email_jordan_formal_complaint_001 (formal complaint, April 25, from jordan.whitfield@brightloopanalytics.com to javier.morales@moveops.com, CC tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com) and email_jordan_wrong_unit_001 (initial complaint, April 22, is_read: false). Content: wrong unit type (unfurnished studio in Montbello instead of furnished 1BR downtown), no UrbanNest contact on site, hotel since April 21 at $180/night, requesting reimbursement. The SENT search returns email_javier_confirms_jordan_move_001 confirming furnished 1BR at 1945 Arapahoe St, Apt 608, flight Southwest 2481 on April 21, with UrbanNest contact promised for key handoff on arrival day.
Search Slack for UrbanNest updates and Jordan Whitfield mention via two separate calls: conversations_search_messages(search_query="Jordan Whitfield") and conversations_search_messages(search_query="UrbanNest"). Key discovery: finds Chloe Vance's message (ts 1776864736, channel C006 #operations) relaying Carmen Reyes' UrbanNest update about a burst pipe in the original building and Jordan being moved to a Montbello studio. The message tags @moveops_suki_patel (not Javier) to notify the traveller. Suki replied in #operations that she was not Jordan's coordinator and treated the message as not an action item for her. This is the root cause: UrbanNest notified MoveOps of the unit swap on approximately April 22, Chloe relayed it internally but tagged the wrong coordinator, and no one reached Jordan before he arrived. MoveOps did know about the change; the failure was internal misdirection, not a missing update from UrbanNest. Also finds Hana Kim's bill entry post in C005 (#finance) confirming the UrbanNest invoice for the original unit, and Javier's earlier confirmation post in C006 (#operations).
Search Airtable for Jordan Whitfield's relocation record via airtable_search_records(base_id="appMoveOpsOps001", table_name="Relocations", field_name="Name", value="Jordan Whitfield"). Returns recReloc00000000035: Name Jordan Whitfield, Company BrightLoop Analytics, Status in Progress, Origin Minneapolis, MN, Destination Denver, CO, Move Start 2026-04-21, Move End 2026-04-23, Assigned Coordinator Javier Morales, Special Requirements 1BR furnished apartment near downtown with economy flight and $1,800 equipment stipend.
Check QuickBooks for Jordan Whitfield financial records via quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_show_data. Finds Invoice Id "1005" with line item "Standard Relocation Package – Jordan Whitfield to Denver" ($4,500), confirming the relocation was invoiced to BrightLoop Analytics. Note: the UrbanNest vendor bill for the original unit (DocNumber UN-DEN-0420-JW, $2,450, covering first month housing $2,100 at 1945 Arapahoe St and $350 move-in fee) is referenced in Hana Kim's email (email_hana_urbannest_bill_jordan_original_unit) as having been entered, but no corresponding bill record exists in QuickBooks yet this bill needs to be created and flagged for dispute since Jordan never occupied the original unit. Hotel reimbursement costs ($180/night from April 21, Staybridge Suites Denver Cherry Creek) are also not yet recorded and require a new QuickBooks bill.
Email UrbanNest (Carmen Reyes) requesting a replacement apartment via send_email(sender="javier.morales@moveops.com", recipients=["carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com"]). The email requests a furnished 1BR near downtown Denver matching Jordan's original specifications (from recReloc00000000035), references the burst pipe and unit swap, and requests a confirmed address plus proper key handoff for the replacement unit.
Reply to Jordan Whitfield's formal complaint confirming reimbursement and next steps via reply_to_email(email_id="email_jordan_formal_complaint_001", sender="javier.morales@moveops.com", content="[apology explaining root cause, confirmation of full hotel reimbursement from April 21 onward, timeline for replacement apartment]", cc=["tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com"]) or send_email(sender="javier.morales@moveops.com", recipients=["jordan.whitfield@brightloopanalytics.com"], cc=["tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com"]). The email apologises, explains what happened (burst pipe caused UrbanNest to swap units, internal notification was misdirected to the wrong coordinator), confirms full hotel reimbursement from April 21 onward, and provides a timeline for the replacement apartment.
Notify Chloe Vance about the incident and its resolution via conversations_add_message(channel_id="C006", payload="[message tagging @moveops_chloe_vance: summary of Jordan Whitfield housing failure, root cause (UrbanNest burst pipe + misdirected notification in her April 22 post), current remediation steps replacement apartment being arranged, hotel reimbursement confirmed, finance notified, Linear ticket created]") or send_email(sender="javier.morales@moveops.com", recipients=["chloe.vance@moveops.com"]). The prompt explicitly says, "Loop in Chloe and anyone else who needs to know."
Update Airtable relocation record via airtable_update_records(base_id="appMoveOpsOps001", table_id="tblRelocations01", records=[{"id": "recReloc00000000035", "fields": {"Status": "In Progress", "Special Requirements": "1BR furnished apartment near downtown. Economy flight. Equipment stipend: $1,800. INCIDENT 2026-04-21: UrbanNest unit swap (burst pipe). Jordan placed in unfurnished Montbello studio. Hotel at Staybridge Suites Denver Cherry Creek from April 21 at $180/night. Replacement unit being arranged via Carmen Reyes. Hotel reimbursement approved. Linear ticket created for tracking."}}]). Keeps Status as "In Progress" (the only applicable valid status while remediation is ongoing) and appends full incident details to the Special Requirements field.
Create a Linear ticket to track the incident and resolution via linear_create_issue(team="Operations", title="Service recovery: Jordan Whitfield Denver housing failure", priority=1). Documents the root cause (burst pipe, misdirected Slack notification to Suki instead of Javier), recovery actions taken (replacement apartment request to Carmen Reyes, hotel reimbursement confirmed to Jordan), and remaining resolution steps (replacement apartment confirmation, hotel bill creation in QuickBooks, UrbanNest original bill dispute).
Notify finance about hotel reimbursement and the disputed UrbanNest bill via conversations_add_message(channel_id="C005", payload="[message to Hana Kim flagging: (1) hotel costs accruing for Jordan Whitfield at Staybridge Suites Denver Cherry Creek from April 21 at $180/night reimbursement approved, please process; (2) UrbanNest DocNumber UN-DEN-0420-JW ($2,450) for the original unit Jordan never occupied needs to be entered as a vendor bill and flagged for dispute or credit from UrbanNest]") or send_email(sender="javier.morales@moveops.com", recipients=["hana.kim@moveops.com"]). This must be a dedicated finance message, not merely tagging Hana in a general operations post.
Create a QuickBooks bill to record the hotel reimbursement obligation via quickbooks_create_bill(VendorRef={"name": "Staybridge Suites Denver Cherry Creek"}, Line=[{"Description": "Hotel reimbursement Jordan Whitfield, Staybridge Suites Denver Cherry Creek, $180/night from April 21 per hotel folio email_jordan_whitfield_hotel_receipt_apr24", "Amount": 900}], DueDate="2026-05-26", TotalAmt=900, Balance=900). The $900 figure represents a minimum of 5 nights at $180 (April 21–25); the hotel folio posted $720 through April 24, with additional nights still accruing the actual amount should be confirmed at checkout and the bill updated accordingly. Formally captures the hotel cost liability in accounts payable so Hana Kim can process reimbursement to Jordan Whitfield. The prompt asks for hotel costs to be "accounted for" finance notification (prior OE) covers communication, but this bill creates the actual financial record.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

1 An email was sent (via send_email or reply_to_email) to [carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com](mailto:carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) requesting a replacement apartment for Jordan Whitfield in Denver, and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

3 Chloe Vance was notified about the Jordan Whitfield housing incident via a Slack message (conversations_add_message) that tags @moveops_chloe_vance, or via an email (send_email) to [chloe.vance@moveops.com](mailto:chloe.vance@moveops.com), and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Chloe Vance was notified in two ways: (1) a conversations_add_message to C006 tagging @moveops_chloe_vance (returned ok:true), and (2) a send_email to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com with chloe.vance@moveops.com as CC. Both tools returned success.
